title: aira caryophyllea var. divaricata
intro: Aira caryophyllea var. divaricata, also known as the Wood Meadow-grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Aira caryophyllea var. divaricata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to treat wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
